response.justification: Candidate is a strong senior/staff individual contributor with broad full-stack experience and some recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at Naver. However, the role requires an executive-level CTO with 5+ years in senior technical leadership, a track record scaling teams from 10 to 30+, production multi-agent AI systems at scale, and deep fintech-infrastructure experience. The resume shows no people-management or org-scaling history, no production multi-agent AI ownership, and only tangential fintech exposure (RIDE Capital, ~15 months as an IC). The gap between IC contributor and CTO is too wide for this specific mandate.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background across backend, distributed systems, and AI-agent/MCP tooling
Some fintech exposure at RIDE Capital plus craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based)
Recent hands-on work with agentic workflows and developer tooling at Naver
concerns: No demonstrated engineering leadership or team-scaling experience (10→30+) required for CTO role
No evidence of owning production multi-agent AI systems in regulated/high-stakes environments
Fintech-infrastructure depth (credit risk, working capital, compliance) is minimal and not at architectural level

key_factors.factor: Lack of executive leadership / team scaling track record
No production multi-agent AI systems ownership
Exposure to agentic AI tooling and MCP frameworks
